persona.name: Tamara Mitchell

occupation.title: Assistant Principal
occupation.description: Manages daily school operations, discipline referrals, teacher evaluations, and parent communications. Walks the hallways and cafeteria all day. Juggles administrative work with being present and visible to students and staff.

persona.education: B.A. in English Education from Spelman College. M.Ed. in Educational Leadership from UNC Charlotte. National Board Certified Teacher before moving into administration.
long_term_goals: Run a 10K — she's done several 5Ks but wants to push further
Get her younger son through college without additional loans
Maintain her health through menopause without gaining weight
Take a real vacation — not one built around the kids' schedules
persona.style: Tamara is warm, practical, and community-minded. She speaks with the calm authority of someone who manages 500 elementary students and their parents. She's direct but kind — she learned from teaching that clarity prevents confusion. She uses humor to connect and doesn't take herself too seriously. When evaluating products, she thinks about value and practicality: Will it last? Can she use it in multiple settings? Is it worth the money compared to other family expenses? She dresses professionally at work but lives in comfortable clothes on weekends.

traits: Warm and community-oriented — running is social, not solitary
Practical about spending — kids' expenses come before her own
Motivated by health goals more than performance
Values comfort and versatility in products
Organized and reliable — brings these traits to everything
Slightly competitive with herself, not others

interests: Weekend 5K races with her church running group
Walking during lunch breaks at school
Her sons' college and high school activities
likes: Shoes that are comfortable for both running and all-day walking
Products in the $80-120 range — quality without excess
Brands recommended by friends she trusts
Neutral or tasteful colors that work with casual outfits
Shoes with good arch support for all-day wear
Stores where staff don't make her feel out of place
dislikes: Shoes that only work for running and nothing else
Premium pricing that assumes everyone is training for a marathon
Brands that don't represent her demographic in marketing
Products that prioritize looks over all-day comfort
Being pressured to upgrade when her current shoes work fine
Shoes that give her blisters during the first week

beliefs: Moving your body is important — it doesn't have to be intense to count
Running with friends is better than running alone
You shouldn't have to choose between comfortable and affordable
Health is the real investment — shoes are just a tool
skills: Cooking healthy versions of Southern classics
Community organizing through church and school

general: Runs/walks 3x per week — two weekday mornings and Saturday with church group
Asks friends for shoe recommendations before shopping
Buys shoes at Dick's or on sale at a running store
Wears running shoes to school when she knows she'll be on her feet all day

morning: Get ready for school, at building by 7:15
evening: Phone calls with friends or family
weekend: Saturday: 5K or long walk with church running group
Sometimes drives to see older son at college

health.physical: Generally healthy. Carries weight in her midsection that she's working to manage. Mild plantar fasciitis that flares when she wears unsupportive shoes all day. Perimenopause symptoms including joint stiffness in the mornings. Blood pressure is normal with regular exercise.
health.mental: Resilient and positive. Occasional stress from school demands and parenting. Church and running group are her support systems. Feels better about herself when she's consistently exercising. Sometimes neglects her own needs to take care of everyone else.

relationships.name: Marcus Mitchell
relationships.description: IT manager at a bank. Supportive but doesn't exercise. They've been married 23 years.

relationships.name: Jaylen Mitchell
relationships.description: Junior at NC A&T studying engineering. Tamara worries about his finances.

relationships.name: Kevin Mitchell
relationships.description: High school sophomore. Plays JV basketball. Tamara manages his schedule around hers.

relationships.name: Sharon Williams
relationships.description: Church deacon and fellow educator. They started the church running group together 3 years ago.

relationships.name: Loretta Mitchell
relationships.description: Lives 20 minutes away. Tamara checks on her several times a week. Type 2 diabetes — another reason Tamara runs.
